question_text: Which statement correctly explains why gravitational field strength decreases with distance from a planet?

options.label: A
options.text: The planet loses mass as objects move further away from it

options.label: B
options.text: The gravitational force spreads out over a larger area as distance increases, so the field strength at any point becomes weaker

options.label: C
options.text: Objects moving away from a planet experience less gravity because they are moving faster

options.label: D
options.text: The atmosphere blocks gravitational field strength, so it weakens above the clouds

explanation: Gravitational field strength is the pull per unit mass, and this spreads out over an increasingly large spherical area as you move away from a planet, similar to how light from a bulb gets dimmer with distance. The planet's mass doesn't change, but its effect is distributed over a larger space.
